intro.dialogue: Before a single note plays, every song you've ever loved already has one thing going: a pulse.

explanation.dialogue: A beat is just a steady pulse - evenly spaced hits in time, like a heartbeat or a clock ticking. Everything else in a song hangs off that skeleton.

example.dialogue: Here's the simplest possible pulse: one kick, spaced perfectly even. Hit play and feel it.

test.dialogue: Your turn. Drag the kick onto the grid so it lands in a steady, even pulse - four hits, same gap every time.
hints: Try spacing the kicks evenly - the same gap between every hit.
Four kicks total, one on every other box: positions 1, 3, 5, and 7.

complete.dialogue: That's the pulse. Everything we build from here sits on top of it.
